question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_BALANCE_FREQUENCY=1ms to override the default Duration frequency of 30 seconds, causing the balance downloader to poll at an extreme rate, exhausting downstream resources and causing fee-relevant balance data to be processed in an inconsistent state, leading to transaction fees computed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a @DurationMin constraint on the frequency field (unlike HistoricalBalanceProperties which enforces @DurationMin(minutes=2)) to inject a near-zero Duration via config, causing the AccountBalancesDownloader @Scheduled task to fire continuously, starving the fee-calculation pipeline of consistent balance snapshots and producing fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a very large Duration value (e.g., Duration.ofDays(365)) into the frequency property via a publicly accessible configuration endpoint, causing balance files to be downloaded so infrequently that fee calculations relying on account balance snapshots use months-old data, resulting in transaction fees computed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.enabled=false via an exposed Spring Boot Actuator /env endpoint, disabling balance file downloads entirely, causing the fee estimation service to operate on stale account balance data and compute transaction fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user call the Lombok-generated setEnabled(false) method through a Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env POST request, toggling the enabled flag at runtime to halt balance ingestion, causing downstream fee calculations to use stale balance snapshots and produce fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user flip persistBytes from false to true via an exposed configuration endpoint, causing raw balance file bytes to be persisted to the database, potentially exposing fee-relevant account balance data that could be used to craft transactions with fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user enable writeFiles=true via a misconfigured configuration source, causing verified balance stream files to be written to the filesystem, potentially allowing the attacker to read or tamper with balance data that feeds into fee calculations, resulting in fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user enable writeSignatures=true via an exposed configuration path, causing signature files to be written to disk, and then exploit filesystem access to replace signature files with crafted ones that validate manipulated balance data, causing fee calculations to use attacker-controlled account balances outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded StreamType.BALANCE return from getStreamType() by manipulating the balance downloader configuration to cause balance stream files to be misrouted or double-processed, resulting in duplicate or incorrect account balance entries that distort fee calculations outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared CommonDownloaderProperties common field to modify the consensusRatio property to a near-zero value, reducing the number of nodes required to agree on a balance file, allowing a minority of nodes to influence balance data used in fee calculations, producing fees outside design parameters?
